generic.intro1: This simulation is designed for those who want to practice with a focus on cloud certifications, with objective questions, immediate feedback and quick review of main topics.
generic.intro2: Throughout this page you'll find a practical study guide, an overview of what is typically tested, and a frequently asked questions section to clarify common doubts before the exam.
generic.topic1: Cloud computing fundamentals (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS, shared responsibility model).
generic.topic2: Identity and Security: IAM, permissions, authentication and basic best practices.
generic.topic3: Core services (compute, storage, networking) and common use cases.
generic.topic4: Costs and billing: how to estimate, optimize and interpret charges.
generic.topic5: Architecture best practices: high availability, resilience and observability.
generic.strategy1: Take 1 short simulation per day and review explanations for wrong answers.
generic.strategy2: Track your progress: reduce time per question without losing accuracy.
generic.strategy3: Re-read the question looking for keywords (always, never, best option, cheapest).
generic.strategy4: Before submitting, review answers you're not confident about.
generic.faq1Answer: Yes. You can start a simulation and practice. In some flows there may be optional registration to save history/statistics.
generic.faq2Question: How many questions should I do per session?
generic.faq2Answer: For consistency, 20–40 questions per session usually works well. To simulate a real exam, use 50–100 questions and maintain focus on time.
generic.faq3Question: Are the questions the same as the official exam?
generic.faq3Answer: No. The goal is to train the same topics and reasoning style — not replicate the official question bank.
generic.faq4Question: What should I do after the simulation?
generic.faq4Answer: Review the explanations, note recurring topics, and retake a simulation focused on those points until you stabilize your performance.

clfC02.intro1: The AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner CLF-C02 simulation is one of the most effective ways to prepare for AWS entry-level certification. Here you practice with questions in the style of the real exam, focused on fundamental cloud, security and cost concepts.
clfC02.intro2: If you're looking for commented AWS Cloud Practitioner questions and want to understand how the exam really tests the content, this simulation is structured to develop reasoning, not just memorization.
clfC02.topic1: AWS shared responsibility model: what is customer responsibility vs AWS responsibility.
clfC02.topic2: IAM in practice: difference between users, groups, roles and policies — and common permission errors.
clfC02.topic3: EC2 vs Lambda: when to use on-demand computing vs serverless.
clfC02.topic4: S3, EBS and EFS: differences between object, block and file storage.
clfC02.topic5: Costs on AWS: how the pay-as-you-go model works and how to avoid unexpected charges.
clfC02.topic6: High availability: using multiple AZs and basic resilience concepts.
clfC02.topic7: CloudFront and CDN: when to use content distribution to reduce latency.
clfC02.topic8: Billing tools: Cost Explorer, Budgets and Trusted Advisor.
clfC02.strategy1: Read the question looking for the main objective: cost, performance, security or simplicity.
clfC02.strategy2: Eliminate clearly wrong alternatives before choosing the best answer.
clfC02.strategy3: When the question is about lowest cost, prioritize managed services and serverless.
clfC02.strategy4: Avoid "overengineering": AWS usually tests the simplest solution that solves the problem.
clfC02.strategy5: If unsure between two options, choose the one requiring less manual management.
clfC02.exampleQuestion: An application needs to store rarely accessed files with the lowest possible cost. Which service is most suitable?
clfC02.exampleExplanation: S3 Glacier is ideal for long-term storage with low cost. Other options are not optimized for rarely accessed file storage.
clfC02.faq1Question: Is the AWS CLF-C02 simulation free?
clfC02.faq1Answer: Yes. You can take the AWS Cloud Practitioner simulation for free and practice with questions similar to the real exam.
clfC02.faq2Question: How many questions are on the AWS Cloud Practitioner exam?
clfC02.faq2Answer: The official exam has approximately 65 questions, with a duration of approximately 90 minutes.
clfC02.faq3Question: What is the minimum passing score for CLF-C02?
clfC02.faq3Answer: The minimum score is 700 on a scale of 100 to 1000.
clfC02.faq4Question: Does this AWS Cloud Practitioner simulation have explained questions?
clfC02.faq4Answer: Yes. The goal is for you to understand why correct and incorrect answers are right, accelerating your learning.
clfC02.faq5Question: How long does it take to prepare for the CLF-C02?
clfC02.faq5Answer: For beginners, 2 to 4 weeks of consistent study with daily simulation practice is usually sufficient.
